entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop whose seed survival is being studied under different storage conditions

entities.name: SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration for which stored seeds remain viable

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS
entities.description: External variables that affect the longevity of stored seeds

entities.name: SEED MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water present in the seed, which significantly influences longevity

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal state of the storage environment affecting seed survival

entities.name: OXYGEN CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The level of oxygen in the storage environment affecting seed survival

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources preserved in seed storage facilities

entities.name: SEED STORAGE FACILITIES
entities.description: Facilities used for the conservation of germplasm

entities.name: ORTHODOX SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds that can tolerate significant desiccation and wide ranges of conditions

entities.name: DESICCATION
entities.description: The process of extreme drying that orthodox seeds can tolerate

entities.name: OILY SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds with high oil content that typically exhibit poorer longevity

entities.name: STARCHY SEEDS
entities.description: Seeds with high starch content that typically exhibit better longevity
